applied_optimizations.name: Scale folding into GEMM weights
applied_optimizations.description: Fold scale parameter into GEMM weights and bias during __init__: weight_scaled = weight * scale.unsqueeze(1), bias_scaled = bias * scale. This eliminates the scale multiplication from the forward pass entirely, saving one elementwise kernel launch. The forward pass is now just GEMM (rocBLAS) + BatchNorm (optimized).

attempted_but_failed.name: Full Triton GEMM + scale + batchnorm
attempted_but_failed.reason: Triton GEMM cannot compete with rocBLAS

attempted_but_failed.name: Triton GEMM + scale with PyTorch BN
PyTorch GEMM with Triton batchnorm
attempted_but_failed.reason: Triton BN adds overhead vs optimized PyTorch BN

attempted_but_failed.name: Runtime scale folding in forward()
attempted_but_failed.reason: Creating scaled weight tensors each forward adds allocation overhead

top_bottlenecks: GEMM operation dominates (~85% of total time)

notes: Key insight: rocBLAS (via PyTorch's nn.Linear) is unbeatable for standard GEMM on MI355X
Scale folding in __init__ eliminates one elementwise operation from forward pass
PyTorch's BatchNorm is highly optimized - Triton implementation adds overhead
